situation: Deep audit audit-2026-07-06-020 found H-001: server.js L84-87 used execAsync with template-literal interpolation of BROWSER_USE_POST_LIMIT env var into shell command string. Attacker-controlled env value could inject arbitrary shell commands. Two other execAsync call sites (L72, L130) used static strings but shared the same unsafe pattern.
action: Replaced all 3 execAsync calls with execFileAsync (no shell parsing). Top-level import changed from exec→execFile; dynamic import in keepalive cron also switched. limit param passed as separate argv element via String(limit). Syntax-checked with node --check before commit.
outcome_evidence: node --check exit=0; opt-worktree.sh commit succeeded; branch auto/opt-sunset-prediction-1783334794 pushed to origin (xtool/wanxia); worktree exists at /home/admin/workspace/.opt-worktrees/sunset-prediction/opt-sunset-prediction-1783334794
next_suggestion: Dispatch H-002+L-003 fix: xhs_keepalive.py .env non-atomic write + SMTP creds hardening
After all 10 pending fixes dispatched or rejected, set audit-cycle-state.status=cycle-complete to trigger next deep audit

audit_findings.finding: Shell injection via template-literal interpolation of env var BROWSER_USE_POST_LIMIT into execAsync command string
audit_findings.remediation: Replaced execAsync with execFileAsync; limit passed as separate argv element
